{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/kogehin/bunzai_20100804143234842.html|title=Bronze triad hanging Buddha|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/rekisisiryo/bunzai_20100726164925399.html|title=Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Okuhikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q11366320|WD=Q11366320}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわしみかしま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市三ケ島)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226968}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|'''Nakahikawa Shrine''' in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nakahikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:中氷川神社(二）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/kogehin/bunzai_20100804143234842.html|title=Bronze triad hanging Buddha|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/rekisisiryo/bunzai_20100726164925399.html|title=Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Okuhikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q11366320|WD=Q11366320}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわしみかしま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市三ケ島)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226968}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|'''Nakahikawa Shrine''' in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nakahikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:中氷川神社(二）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/kogehin/bunzai_20100804143234842.html|title=Bronze triad hanging Buddha|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/rekisisiryo/bunzai_20100726164925399.html|title=Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633}} *{{ill|lt=Okuhikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} *{{ill|lt=Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q11366320|WD=Q11366320}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかひかわしんしや ところさわしみかしま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakahikawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の氷川神社]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:所沢市の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/kogehin/bunzai_20100804143234842.html|title=Bronze triad hanging Buddha|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/rekisisiryo/bunzai_20100726164925399.html|title=Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/kogehin/bunzai_20100804143234842.html|title=Bronze triad hanging Buddha|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nakahikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:中氷川神社(二）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite {'text': '埼玉県所沢市三ケ島5-1691-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市三ケ島)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226968}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|'''Nakahikawa Shrine''' in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nakahikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:中氷川神社(二）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=New Compilation of Musashi Fudoki|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.159 Iruma District No.8|chapter=Mikajima Village Nagamiya Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/kogehin/bunzai_20100804143234842.html|title=Bronze triad hanging Buddha|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/rekisisiryo/bunzai_20100726164925399.html|title=Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} *{{ill|lt=Okuhikawa Shrine|Q11445942|WD=Q11445942|1=Oku-Hikawa Shrine}} *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nakahikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:中氷川神社(二）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite {'text': '埼玉県所沢市三ケ島5-1691-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県所沢市山口', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:中氷川神社 (所沢市三ケ島)]] {{wikidata link|Q17226968}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|'''Nakahikawa Shrine''' in Mikajima, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine in Yamaguchi, Tokorozawa|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nakahikawa Shrine |画像 = [[File:中氷川神社(二）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}Mikajima 5-1691-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Kasuya)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Kasuya * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tokorozawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayashi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tokorozawa Higashisayamagaoka)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Higashisayamagaoka === Notes === {{reflist}} === References Myōjin-sha|id={{NDLJP|764001/83}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/index.html|title=Designated cultural properties of Tokorozawa|website=Tokorozawa City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|ref={{sfnref|所沢市の指定文化財}}}} === External links === *{{Cite website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-3-7}} *{{Cite City website|accessdate=2022-11-27|date=2019-10-16}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} *{{ill|lt=Hikawa Shrine|Q703633|WD=Q703633|1=Hikawa Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Traditionally 1st century BCE |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|WD=Q2497|1=April 15}}、{{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|WD=Q2882|1=September 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=47|lat_sec=22|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=52|label=Nakahikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|47|22|N|139|23|52|E}}}} }} {{commonscat|Naka-hikawa-jinja (Mikajima, Tokorozawa)|Nakahikawa Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)}} '''Nakahikawa Shrine'''（なかひかわじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Hikawa faith|Q11549596|WD=Q11549596|1=Hikawa shinkō}} in {{ill|lt=Mikajima (Tokorozawa)|Q14085473|WD=Q14085473|lt=Mikajima|1=Mikajima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokorozawa|Q165857|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the tutelary shrine of former Mikajima village and was formerly a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === It is said to have been enshrined from Omiya Hikawa Shrine in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} ({{ill|lt=97 BCE|Q41672|WD=Q41672|1=97 BC}}-{{ill|lt=29 BCE|Q40835|WD=Q40835|1=29 BC}}), and that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} stopped here during his eastern campaign and enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} it was called "Nakamiyā," and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Nagamiyā Myōjin-sha" because of its long, narrow precincts{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 三ヶ島村}}. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted it shuin land. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed Nakahikawa Shrine. Since Meiji, it has claimed to be the candidate for the Shikinaisha Nakahikawa-sha, but this has not gained strong support. === Buildings and precincts === [[File:Naka-Hikawa-jinja (Tokorozawa Mikajima) 01.jpg|thumb|220px|The torii is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinmei torii|1=UNKNOWN}} type]] * A sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} that fell and died in a storm soon after the war has been preserved. * There is also a poem monument for {{ill|lt=Mikajima Yoshiko|Q11354060|WD=Q11354060|1=Yoshiko Mikashima}}, a poet and daughter of a priest of this shrine. ** "Spring rain - from the hazy boughs of the oak, drops of dew glitter and fall." === Festivals === * The spring grand festival is held on April 15, and the autumn grand festival on September 29. * A {{ill|lt=horse-back streaming race|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} ritual used to be held on the long approach, but it has ceased. === Cultural properties === * Bronze triad hanging Buddha: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Craftwork){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. * Nichikarin-ō-no-hi monument: designated cultural property of Tokorozawa (Tangible Cultural Property / Historical Material){{sfn|所沢市の指定文化財}}. === Location and access === * Mikajima 5-1691-1, Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture ** From {{ill|lt=Kotesashi Station|Q6433936|WD=Q6433936|1=Kotesashi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Seibu Ikebukuro Line|Q93546|WD=Q93546|1=Seibu Ikebukuro Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Seibu Bus|Q11628880|WD=Q11628880|1=Seibu Bus}} route {{ill|lt=Kote 04/06|Q11628891|WD=Q11628891|1=Kote 04/06}}, get off at Horinouchi. === Affiliated shrines === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman Shrine (Tokorozawa Mikajima)|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} - Tokorozawa, Mikajima *
